news.title: RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25% amid West Asia crisis
news.para: The Reserve Bank of India has maintained its interest rates for the second consecutive time. This decision comes as the ongoing West Asia conflict disrupts energy supplies and raises crude oil prices. These factors create fiscal and inflationary pressures for India. The Monetary Policy Committee decided to keep the repo rate at 5.25 percent.

news.title: ​Circle rates surge in villages along expressway corridor in Lucknow
news.para: Uttar Pradesh government has revised circle rates for agricultural and non-agricultural land in Lucknow district, particularly near proposed expressway corridors. The updated valuations, effective Thursday, aim to align government rates with market prices, impacting land transactions and stamp duty collections.

news.title: Visakhapatnam: GVMC commissioner orders faster LRS, TDR, building approval process
news.para: Visakhapatnam GVMC Commissioner Ketan Garg has ordered town planning officials to expedite building permissions, LRS applications, and TDR approvals, warning of strict action against delays and corruption. He emphasized transparency and prompt processing of applications submitted through the APDPMS portal, urging field verification and disposal of LRS applications without delay.

news.title: Vacate ahead of monsoon, Pune civic body tells occupants of 36 dilapidated wadas
news.para: Pune Municipal Corporation has issued notices to 36 properties to vacate dilapidated wadas before the monsoon. Despite a decade-old initiative to grant tenancy certificates, only a fraction of eligible tenants have received them, with many hesitant to leave due to fear of losing their homes and lack of rehabilitation assurances.

news.title: Over 300 houses regularised under Mhaje Ghar scheme in Goa
news.para: Goa government has regularized 300 illegal houses under the Mhaje Ghar scheme, urging more applications. Chief Minister Pramod Sawant announced over 10,000 applications are pending, with sanads to be issued soon. The scheme, launched in October 2025, aims to regularize over 50,000 illegal structures.

news.title: Lucknow development body to launch three residential schemes in June
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority is launching three major residential schemes this month: Naimish Nagar, Varun Vihar, and Wellness City. These projects aim to meet housing demand and support urban expansion, with registration for over 2,100 plots in Naimish Nagar's first phase commencing soon. All allotments will be via lottery.

news.title: Were officials who cleared illegal buildings ever held accountable, asks Telangana HC
news.para: The Telangana High Court questioned officials regarding approvals for illegal constructions on government land. Residents of Patelguda village had their homes demolished by HYDRAA. A later survey confirmed the lands were private patta lands. Despite this, municipal authorities are refusing building permissions. The court has directed respondents to file counters and will hear the matter again.

news.title: Fire safety checks mandatory for offices, malls and hotels in Gurugram
news.para: Gurgaon administration has made fire safety audits mandatory for all commercial buildings. This directive follows a devastating hotel fire in Delhi. The fire department will check firefighting equipment, emergency exits, and evacuation plans. Compliance with fire safety norms is essential to prevent tragedies. Violations will lead to action and immediate corrective measures.

news.title: Cement demand growth may moderate to mid-single digits in FY27: India Ratings
news.para: India Ratings has maintained a neutral outlook on the cement sector for FY27, expecting demand growth to moderate to mid-single digits from about 8% in FY26. The agency said rising fuel, freight and packing material costs may reduce EBITDA per tonne by around 15% year-on-year, while tier-2 cement players could face greater pressure due to lower pricing power and limited financial headroom.

news.title: Andheri set for Mumbai’s first 100-acre slum cluster redevelopment
news.para: Mumbai's largest slum redevelopment project, the Juhu Gulli SRA scheme, is seeing a competitive bidding war. Three major corporate players have submitted bids for the 100-acre project, aiming to transform slum hutments and old buildings. This initiative promises enhanced transparency and a better living standard for residents.

news.title: Not interfering in realty firm land in Kukatpally: HYDRAA informs Telangana HC
news.para: HYDRAA informed the Telangana high court that it is no longer interfering with a 13.17-acre land in Kukatpally, previously in possession of NVN Constructions Pvt Ltd. The agency had previously demolished structures on the site, claiming it was part of a waterbody, despite an undertaking that it was private patta land.

news.title: DDA extends last date of booking under its Towering Heights residential scheme
news.para: The Delhi Development Authority has extended the booking period for its 'DDA Towering Heights' housing scheme in Karkardooma, East Delhi, until June 30. This extension offers a further chance for individual buyers and government agencies to purchase flats in Delhi's tallest residential building, a 48-storey tower developed under the city's Transit Oriented Development policy.

news.title: Jaipur developer moves SC over alleged commercial use of residential land, seeks directions to civic authorities
news.para: The application has been filed in the pending matter of Loganathan vs State of Tamil Nadu & Ors., in which the Supreme Court, in March this year, directed municipal bodies across all state capitals to identify residential areas allegedly being used for commercial purposes and submit compliance reports.

news.title: Develop 11 mega industrial parks, create 50,000-acre land bank: Bihar CM
news.para: Bihar's Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary has directed the development of 11 mega industrial parks and food parks in all 38 districts to boost industrial growth and employment. The state aims to create a land bank of 50,000 acres and simplify procedures to become an attractive investment destination, focusing on sectors like food processing and textiles.

news.title: Maharashtra government revises base rates under sand extraction policy
news.para: Maharashtra government has revised sand extraction policy, introducing new base rates and royalty for eco-friendly sand derived from mining by-products. This initiative aims to reduce pressure on riverbeds and encourage industries producing sand from overburden, while also curbing illegal excavation and supporting the construction sector.

news.title: UP-RERA says over 1.15 lakh units available across Uttar Pradesh
news.para: UP-RERA has advised homebuyers and investors to make property purchase decisions after verifying project details and assessing their financial capacity. The authority said more than 1.15 lakh residential and commercial units are currently available across Uttar Pradesh, and buyers should not act under pressure created by claims of stock shortage or imminent price hikes.

news.title: NGT sets up panel to probe ‘illegal sand mining in Rushikulya river’
news.para: The National Green Tribunal has ordered a joint investigation into alleged illegal sand mining and environmental violations at a Rushikulya river sand quarry. Notices have been issued to state authorities and regulatory agencies following a petition highlighting irregularities, including mechanized extraction and exceeding lease areas. A committee will examine the site and recommend measures within a month.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh government to draft GIS-based master plans for 93 tier-II towns across 49 districts
news.para: Uttar Pradesh is preparing GIS-based master plans for 93 tier II towns with populations between 50,000 and 1 lakh to ensure planned development. This initiative aims to detail land usage and future projects for government agencies and stakeholders, addressing a gap in regulatory oversight for many of these urban local bodies.

news.title: Property tax defaulters in Chandigarh to soon get recovery notices
news.para: Chandigarh civic body is preparing to send recovery notices to approximately 70,000-75,000 property tax defaulters. These notices will include penalties and interest on unpaid taxes. Residential property defaulters are identified as the majority. Stern actions, including property sealing for commercial defaulters and water supply disconnection for residential defaulters, will be taken if taxes remain unpaid.
